title: RT-1: Robotics Transformer for Real-World Control at Scale
abstract: We study the ability of Large Behavior Models (LBMs) to exhibit task generalization and robustness when trained at scale. To that end, we develop RT-1, a Robotics Transformer (RT) that can absorb a large amount of real-world demonstrations when trained at scale and exhibits positive transfer, meaning that tasks with more data benefit other tasks. RT-1 uses an efficient Transformer-based architecture that tokenizes task descriptions and images and produces discretized action tokens. It can be trained end-to-end on data from multiple robots and demonstrations of various skills.
introduction_excerpt: We present RT-1, a Robotics Transformer model that incorporates over 130k episodes, covering 700+ tasks, trained on a real robotic system. RT-1 consists of a pretrained EfficientNet-B3 image encoder conditioned on a task description via FiLM, a TokenLearner to compress image tokens, and a Transformer to map these tokens to discretized actions.
